project.name: luna-ztrust
project.description: Ephemeral SSH authentication for AI agents, DevOps runners, and automation via a self-hosted luna-proxy that holds signing keys in an in-memory keystore, issues short-lived SSH certificates or hosted-key signatures, and gates access with out-of-band approval. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.description: Publishable luna-sdk library: mTLS client, PoP, HMAC, cert/signature HTTP client, and signer helpers consumed by luna-agent.

layers.name: Client Agent
layers.description: SSH_AUTH_SOCK daemon that blocks on Sign via SDK; config loading, identity selection, and agent entrypoint in agent/.

layers.name: Proxy HTTP API
layers.description: mTLS HTTP surface and auth pipeline: sign/wait handlers, CLI/mobile/admin routes, Telegram webhook, and HMAC/PoP/replay checks.

layers.name: Proxy Core Services
layers.description: In-memory keystore and signing (local-ca/local-key), approval queue, session leases, mobile device registry, and CLI enrollment/key-load.

layers.name: Proxy Control Plane
layers.description: Unix control socket with SO_PEERCRED admin auth: status, key load/confirm/reject, mobile and CLI operator commands.

layers.name: Proxy CLI Commands
layers.description: luna-proxy Cobra CLI: serve, key management, mobile/CLI subcommands, and operator output formatting in proxy/cmd.

layers.name: Proxy Configuration
layers.description: Proxy YAML/TOML loading, mTLS cert paths, merge rules, and example deployment configs for luna-proxy.

layers.description: Go workspace root, Makefile, GitHub Actions, Goreleaser, Docker E2E compose, deploy scripts, test CA fixtures, and env templates.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to learn what Luna Z-Trust is: ephemeral SSH certificate authentication for AI agents and automation via a self-hosted luna-proxy, luna-agent SSH_AUTH_SOCK daemon, and publishable luna-sdk client library. The README maps the operator control socket, remote key load workflow, build commands, and the mTLS HTTP API surface you will follow through the rest of this tour.

tour.title: Three-Module Architecture
tour.description: AGENTS.md and go.work define the strict module layout: sdk (client protocol), proxy (control plane, never imports sdk), and agent (SSH daemon, imports sdk only). Understanding these dependency rules explains why HTTP, PoP, HMAC, and mTLS live in one place while the proxy handles Telegram approval and in-memory signing keys separately.

tour.title: Proxy Bootstrap
tour.description: The luna-proxy binary starts at main.go and delegates runtime wiring to the serve subcommand. serve.go loads configuration, constructs the approval store, auth replay LRU, sealed keystore, signing backends, mTLS HTTP API server, and Unix control socket — giving you a single map of every subsystem the proxy runs concurrently.
tour.languageLesson: Go build tags like //go:build linux gate platform-specific control socket code (SO_PEERCRED) while keeping the same serve wiring on all platforms.

tour.title: SDK Client Protocol
tour.description: Building on the module split from Step 2, luna-sdk is the single protocol implementation clients use. client.go exposes RequestCertificate and RequestSignature; sign/client.go implements the POST /api/v1/ssh/sign plus wait polling flow with mTLS, HMAC body MAC, and proof-of-possession binding to target user and IP.

tour.title: SSH Agent Daemon
tour.description: luna-agent implements the OpenSSH agent protocol on a Unix socket. agent.go blocks Sign until the SDK AccessProvider returns an ephemeral certificate or hosted-key signature; main.go loads YAML config, wires the SDK client, resolves identities from proxy capabilities, and starts the agent listener.

tour.title: Sign Flow & Auth Pipeline
tour.description: When an agent or automation client calls POST /api/v1/ssh/sign, sign_handler.go creates a transaction and exposes the tx/wait pattern. Before any tx_id is issued, pipeline.go runs a fail-closed auth chain — mTLS, HMAC, timestamp, replay LRU, then PoP — and store.go tracks approval state until signing completes or times out.

tour.title: Telegram OOB Approval
tour.description: After auth succeeds, telegram.go sends out-of-band approval prompts and the keystore design spec documents the sealed-until-key-load operator model. Transactions stay pending until an operator approves via Telegram (or LUNA_ENV=dev auto-approve); only then does the approval store issue credentials through the configured signing backend.

tour.title: In-Memory Keystore
tour.description: Signing keys never touch disk on the proxy. keystore.go holds a sealed gate until an operator loads encrypted PEM material; pool.go manages the local-key fingerprint pool; local_ca.go issues user certificates while local_key.go signs agent-provided host-key data. POST /api/v1/ssh/sign returns 503 until a signer is loaded.

tour.title: CLI Remote Key Load
tour.description: Enrolled luna-cli devices (OU=luna-cli mTLS only) can upload encrypted host keys without local socket access. cli_handler.go serves POST /api/v1/cli/keys/load; load.go is the mTLS HTTP client the proxy CLI uses to send encrypted PEM; the design spec defines enrollment, rate limits, and local-key-only constraints.

tour.title: Unix Control Socket
tour.description: Local operators manage the proxy through a Linux Unix socket authenticated via SO_PEERCRED. server.go accepts JSON requests; ops.go implements status, key.load/list/remove/confirm/reject, and CLI/mobile admin; key.go exposes the same operations as Cobra commands for day-to-day keystore and device management.
